title: Emit Kubernetes Events for key lifecycle transitions
summary: Implementation adds 19 unique Kubernetes event reasons (20 Recorder calls) across 5 sub-reconciler files: bootstrap (2 events), database non-upgrade (3), database upgrade (11 reasons across 12 calls), fernet (1), credential (1), deployment (1). Events are emitted alongside existing condition-setting code using r.Recorder.Event/Eventf with Normal type for success and Warning type for failures. No events are emitted for polling/in-progress states. Unit tests verify event emission for every code path using FakeRecorder with Receive/ContainSubstring assertions, plus negative assertions for polling states. Reference documentation at docs/reference/keystone-events.md covers all 19 event reasons with type, trigger condition, example messages, alerting configuration examples, and an event flow diagram. VitePress sidebar updated.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass for all event-emitting code paths
Positive assertions verify correct event type and reason for each lifecycle transition
Negative assertions verify no events emitted for polling/in-progress states
FakeRecorder pattern consistent with existing test infrastructure
Edge cases covered (stale job detection, tag change during upgrade, version parse errors)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (Recorder calls alongside condition-setting)
Event reason strings are stable PascalCase identifiers
Normal type for success, Warning type for failure - no exceptions
No events for polling states to avoid noise

security_checklist.item: Events RBAC marker (core/events create;patch) already present pre-PR
No user input passed unsanitized to event messages
No secrets or sensitive data exposed in event messages

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal - single Recorder call per transition
Existing Recorder field on KeystoneReconciler properly utilized

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No abstraction layer - direct Recorder calls

fail_fast_checklist.item: Error events emitted immediately at point of failure
Event messages include error details for debugging

defensive_checklist.item: No external inputs to validate (events use internal state only)

detected_patterns.name: Kubernetes event emission alongside condition-setting with FakeRecorder test assertions
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new sub-reconciler or lifecycle transition that sets a status condition on the Keystone CR (or future operator CRs following the same pattern)
detected_patterns.description: Every significant lifecycle transition (success or failure) that sets a status condition via conditions.SetCondition also emits a Kubernetes event via r.Recorder.Event/Eventf. Normal type for success, Warning type for failure. No events for polling/in-progress states. Event reasons are stable PascalCase strings matching the condition Reason. Tests use record.NewFakeRecorder(10) and assert event emission with g.Expect(fakeRecorder.Events).To(Receive(ContainSubstring("Type Reason"))) for positive cases and g.Expect(fakeRecorder.Events).ToNot(Receive()) for negative cases (polling states).

examples.code: r.Recorder.Eventf(keystone, corev1.EventTypeWarning, "BootstrapFailed", "Keystone bootstrap job failed: %v", err)
